channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Israeli defense minister orders IDF to plan for Gazans to leave in line with Trump's controversial proposal
item.description: Defense Minister Israel Katz on Thursday ordered the IDF to prepare a plan for residents of Gaza who wish to leave and applauded President Donald Trumps "bold plan.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US officials now say Trump only wants to displace Palestinians from Gaza temporarily
item.description: GUATEMALA CITY (AP) — President Donald Trump’s top diplomat and his main spokesperson on Wednesday walked back the idea that he wants the permanent relocation of Palestinians from Gaza, after American allies and even Republican lawmakers rebuffed his suggestion that the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US to seize Venezuelan president's plane held in Dominican Republic during Rubio visit
item.description: The Trump administration plans to seize the second of two planes belonging to Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro's government that is currently in the Dominican Republic.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Panama eliminates charge fees for U.S. government vessels to use the canal
item.description: Panama eliminates charge fees for U.S. government vessels after meeting with Secretary of State Marco Rubio.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In their own words: What Trump said about Gaza and how top administration officials contradicted him
item.description: President Donald Trump's comments that the U.S. should take long-term control of war-shattered Gaza and that the area's residents should be permanently resettled have been contradicted by top people in his administration.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump OPM buyout offers leave federal workers with risky decisions
item.description: The Trump administration and federal employee unions are in a head-to-head lobbying battle as workers face a Feb. 6 deadline to decide whether they want to accept a government buyout.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Musk says DOGE will make 'rapid safety upgrades' to air traffic control system
item.description: "With the support of President @realDonaldTrump, the @DOGE team will aim to make rapid safety upgrades to the air traffic control system," Musk wrote Wednesday on the social platform X.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Callers swamp Senate offices in Trump's first weeks, leading to busy signals and full voicemail
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Callers are getting busy signals and voicemail inboxes are full at many U.S. Senate offices as people try to reach out and voice their opinions on President Donald Trump’s Cabinet picks, executive orders and moves to dismantle various federal programs.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Michigan's Arab American community offers muted response to Trump's Gaza takeover plan
item.description: DEARBORN, Mich. (AP) — Residents of the largest Arab American community in the U.S. had plenty to say during the 2024 presidential campaign about the roiling politics in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI and MIKE HOUSEHOLDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: People gather at Minnesota Capitol in nationwide protest against President Trump
item.description: Protesters marched to the Capitol from St. Paul College, carrying signs denouncing policies on immigration, LGBTQ+ rights and federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: Regina Medina and Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Walz sets special election for seat in suburban Ramsey County that will decide House power
item.description: A special election will fill a seat won by a Democrat in the November election that came open after a residency challenge. The result could return the Minnesota House to a tie or boost a GOP majority.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: DOGE actions 'long overdue, much welcomed': House speaker
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson is defending the Elon Musk-led DOGE, saying the government needs to spend money better.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: With Gaza rehab and other global policy ideas, Trump goes from America First to America Everywhere
item.description: President Donald Trump promised voters an administration that wouldn’t waste precious American lives and taxpayer treasure on far-off wars and nation building.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani And Zeke Miller, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Stauber reintroduces bill to reverse mining ban near Boundary Waters, return leases to Twin Metals
item.description: Northern Minnesota Republican Congressman Pete Stauber has reintroduced legislation to overturn a 20-year mining ban near the Boundary Waters Canoe Area Wilderness. If passed, the bill would also return mineral leases to Twin Metals, the company vying to build an underground copper mine near Ely.
item.dc:creator: Dan Kraker, MPR News

item.title: McConnell slips and falls outside Senate Chamber
item.description: Former Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) slipped and fell on the stairs leaving the Senate Chamber on Wednesday afternoon, prompting concerned aides to rush to help the 82-year-old lawmaker.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: USAID staffers stunned, angered by Trump admin's DOGE shutdown of $40B agency
item.description: Staffers at the U.S. Agency for International Development were stunned and angered on Monday after President Donald Trump and cost cutter Elon Musk shut down the $40 billion agency.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Freshman Democrat, physician Rep. Morrison warns against RFK Jr. nomination
item.description: Minnesota Third-District Democratic Rep. Kelly Morrison, who is one of a handful of physicians in Congress, joined Morning Edition host Cathy Wurzer to express her concerns and what Kennedy’s confirmation could mean in practice.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: CIA offers buyouts to staffers as new director looks to stamp Trump's imprint on the agency
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The CIA confirmed on Wednesday that it has offered buyouts to employees who voluntarily resign, the latest group of workers targeted in President Donald Trump's push to transform the federal government.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge cements block on Trump’s order restricting birthright citizenship
item.description: GREENBELT, Md. — A federal judge Wednesday furthered a block of President Trump’s executive order preventing the children of migrants without legal status from receiving birthright citizenship. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: The first group of migrants has been sent to Guantánamo, but legal challenges loom
item.description: President Trump aims to deport up to 30,000 migrant detainees to a holding facility there, despite the questionable legality of that move.
item.dc:creator: Sacha Pfeiffer, MPR News

item.title: Senate Dems ask Hegseth to probe potential price gouging on military families' housing
item.description: “The Department of Defense has a responsibility to protect military families from predatory private housing companies.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Shutdown fears grow as Trump battles multiply
item.description: The odds of a government shutdown are surging as President Trump battles Democrats over efforts to freeze funding and Republicans brawl internally over the size and scope of potential cuts.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans in Congress mostly shrug as Musk and DOGE set sights on spending
item.description: Congress controls the power of the purse, but Republicans on Capitol Hill have put up little resistance to efforts by the administration to suspend spending that they've already approved.
item.dc:creator: Deirdre Walsh, MPR News

item.title: Race for NYC mayor heats up as primary opponents hit Adams on education, immigrants
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (PIX11) -- During a budget hearing in front of the state legislature Tuesday, two Democrats looking to unseat fellow Democratic Mayor Eric Adams took their shot. State Sen.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Nevada prisons director points to 4-mile fence, better staffing; inmate food issues persist
item.description: A high-profile inmate escape, the resignation of the prisons director, and reports that fences, doors and windows were vulnerable. It all added up to a spotlight on the Nevada Department of Corrections in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 5 things to know about Trump's plan to create a sovereign wealth fund
item.description: President Donald Trump is taking steps to overhaul the way the U.S. government invests its money and change the nature of America’s financial presence abroad.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, Fox 5

item.title: Schools poised to be major front line in Trump immigration crackdown
item.description: Oklahoma is moving to require proof of citizenship for the guardians of public school students, while California schools are distributing "know your rights" cards to immigrant families as K-12 campuses become a crucial front line in President Trump's immigration crackdown.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Local congressman reacts to inspectors general dismissed by President Trump
item.description: In the flurry of Trump's executive orders covering immigration raids, federal firings, funding freezes and tariff negotiations, the dismissing of 17 inspector generals has gotten little notice, but according to San Diego Congressman Mike Levin, this is a bigger issue than most understand.
item.dc:creator: Jaime Chambers, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada Senate was a battleground in 2023, new Republican minority leader emphasizes setting priorities
item.description: The Nevada Senate is ready to get down to business, and the end of the 2023 Legislature is still fresh in the minds of Democrats and Republicans gathered in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: John Langeler, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: President Donald Trump agrees to pause tariffs on Mexico
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum said Monday that after a conversation with U.S. President Donald Trump that the planned tariffs are on hold for a month, a statement confirmed by the White House.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: DeSantis, Florida GOP tensions spill out into open
item.description: Tensions are spilling out into the open between Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis (R) and members of his own party in the Sunshine State, pointing to a new discordant era of GOP-led politics in a state that has trended red over the last few cycles.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ken Martin elected next DNC chair
item.description: Minnesota state party chair Ken Martin was elected on Saturday as the next leader of the Democratic National Committee (DNC) after securing a majority of the 448 DNC committee members’ votes.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Air traffic controllers union responds to Trump's DEI attacks
item.description: The air traffic controllers union responded Friday to claims by President Trump this week that diversity programs contributed to the fatal crash in Washington Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Giorno, Fox 5

item.title: Who is Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy? You may recognize him from MTV
item.description: Duffy, formerly a reality TV star and Wisconsin congressman, was confirmed as the new transportation secretary on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Jill Colvin, Fox 5
